title: People languishing in jails because of judiciary's ‘more loyal than the king syndrome’: Supreme Court Justice Ujjal Bhuyan
description: Supreme Court judge Justice Ujjal Bhuyan on Sunday warned that a “more loyal than the king syndrome” within parts of the judiciary has contributed to prolonged incarceration of accused persons.He said such tendencies result in people remaining in jail for months and years, particularly in cases involving stringent laws.“Many within the judiciary suffer from this more loyal than the king syndrome. As a result, people continue to languish in jails for months and months together,” Justice Bhuyan sa
